title: Timeout Layers, Deadlines, Cancellation, Cleanup, and Budget Allocation · Go Client Systems · Language Lens: Cancellation Cleanup
summary: A health aggregator has a 900 ms caller deadline spanning DNS, TLS, headers, streamed content, parsing, and cleanup. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http go cancellation cleanup increment in a Go 1.26 net/http end-to-end request budget and cancellation model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Stop body processing, producers, timers, and queued work after cancellation and release every owned resource. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Allocate an end-to-end latency budget across attempts and phases while preserving time for cleanup and fallback. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Predict Cancellation Cleanup · Theory Cancellation Cleanup
steps.instruction:
theory case — Go fleet evidence model has 900 ms end-to-end for DNS, connect, TLS, headers, streamed body, parse, and cleanup. A caller cancels during body read; allocate phase budgets, propagate the reason, stop owned work, and preserve unknown remote effect. Competency probe: Stop body processing, producers, timers, and queued work after cancellation and release every owned resource. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Cancellation Cleanup contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Go Predict Cancellation Cleanup · Theory Cancellation Cleanup.
